titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Usserianus secundus
VL28
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Mc Lc Jo

surrogates.bibl: Black and white negative photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.

listBibl.bibl: T.K. Abbott, Evangeliorum versio antehieronymiana ex codice Usseriano (Dublinensi) adiecta collatione codicis Usseriani alterius, Dublin 1884.
H.C. Hoskier, The text of Codex Usserianus 2. r2. ("Garland of Howth") with critical notes to supplement and correct the collation of the late T.K. Abbott, London 1919.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers pages 163-172.. It contains John 5:12-10:3.The transcription includes foliation, lineation and punctuation. The manuscript uses a number of abbreviations, including those for 'autem', 'est', 'enim', 'dixit', 'respondit' and so on. These words have all been transcribed in full, although nasal bars have been recorded. Lacunae are normally indicated by the number of characters missing. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the revised plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
The file was revised by HH to incorporate changes arising from Philip Burton's consultation of the manuscript in Dublin on 27th and 28th June 2012.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Change to verse division of 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: Pages 160-162 of the manuscript contain Luke 22:24-23:14
Hoskier only reads the initial 's...' of 'scri(psit)'
Autem, abbreviated as hr, apparently just visible. Hoskier does not record it.
superline with Hoskier. It is not certain in the photograph
The surviving pages of the manuscript jump from John 6:25 ending page 166 to John 8:7 beginning page 167.
The abbreviation 'pri:' is expanded following Fischer 1991
Hoskier does not include ei ih¬s
For "lux huius mundi" Abbott reads "huius mundi lumen", but the manuscript images are clear.
Gap on page; no sign of expected 'meum' or erasure
We have followed the reading 'A liliberi' of Hoskier and Abbott, although 'Et liliberi' is a possible alternative based on the photographs.
Hoskier did not read the first three letters of 'samaritanus'
For "dixerunt ei" Abbott reads "dixerunt ei iudei"
Hoskier could not read "quid fecit tibi"
Hoskier could not read "ant et qui"
